_description: Curated knob set for fine-grained response-curve characterization. Tier 1 = direct power/DVFS levers. Power-cap knobs swept in 10 % steps. Frequency knobs use literal-Hz levels (post-8509922 verification) instead of fraction_range, since fraction_range relied on AVAIL signals that returned n/a on Aurora and on writes that were silently clamped by MIN_CONTROL. The frequency knobs now also carry a min_control field telling the sweep runner to drop the corresponding MIN_CONTROL to its floor before each MAX write -- without this every MAX write below the existing MIN is silently ignored. Tier 2 = secondary effects, swept at 2 levels (half/double).
_total_cells_per_bench_variant_repeat: 66 (57 Tier1 + 9 Tier2); ~22 min wall at 20 s/cell.
_repeats_strategy: Per-cell repeats=1; cross-PBS repeats=10 via scripts/submit_phase0_all_repeat.sh. n=10 samples per (bench, knob, level) -> std-err < 1% energy.

knobs.name: BOARD_POWER_LIMIT_CONTROL
knobs._rationale: Whole-node PL1 cap; the headline lever for cap-compliance experiments. Aurora's BOARD_POWER_LIMIT_CONTROL defaults to 0.0 (no cap) so we use literal watts. Range spans [non-binding 5500W, headline 3000W, aggressive 1500W]. Step ~500W gives 9-point curve.

knobs.name: CPU_POWER_LIMIT_CONTROL
knobs._rationale: RAPL package PL1 (CPU socket cap). Scaled from CPU_POWER_LIMIT_DEFAULT (TDP) so the curve transfers across SKUs. 9 sample points in 10 % steps.

knobs.name: DRAM_POWER_LIMIT_CONTROL
knobs._rationale: DRAM/HBM RAPL cap. Defaults are non-zero on Aurora so scale_readback works. Expected small impact (DRAM ~1 % of node power) but characterize the full curve to confirm.

knobs.name: CPU_FREQUENCY_MAX_CONTROL
knobs._rationale: Primary CPU DVFS lever. Verified via results/8509922: every literal Hz from 1.0 GHz to 3.5 GHz binds exactly (readback == requested, STATUS rock-steady) PROVIDED CPU_FREQUENCY_MIN_CONTROL is dropped to MIN_AVAIL first -- otherwise MAX writes below the existing MIN are silently clamped. min_control config tells the sweep runner to drop MIN to 0.8 GHz before writing MAX.

knobs.name: CPU_UNCORE_FREQUENCY_MAX_CONTROL
knobs._rationale: Uncore DVFS lever (mesh, LLC, memory controllers). Critical for memory-bound workloads. Verified via results/8509922: 0.8/1.2/1.6/2.0 GHz bind exactly; 2.4 GHz request lands at 2.3 GHz silicon max. Min_control drops UNCORE_MIN to 0.8 GHz first.

knobs.name: GPU_CORE_FREQUENCY_MAX_CONTROL
knobs._rationale: Primary GPU DVFS lever on Aurora (GPU power cap not writable). BREAKTHROUGH from results/8509922: every literal from 0.4 GHz to 1.4 GHz binds EXACTLY when GPU_CORE_FREQUENCY_MIN_CONTROL is dropped to 0.2 GHz first. The Phase 0 'PVC busy floor at 1.5 GHz' theory was wrong -- it was a MIN_CONTROL pin from the driver default. With min_control config the agent can cap PVC tiles down to 0.4 GHz for huge GPU-idle energy savings.

knobs.name: LEVELZERO::GPU_CORE_PERFORMANCE_FACTOR_CONTROL
knobs._rationale: PVC Performance Factor: 0.0 favors power saving (mem-biased, slower kernels), 1.0 favors max performance (compute-biased). 7 sample points to map both endpoints + intermediate biases.

knobs.name: BOARD_POWER_TIME_WINDOW_CONTROL
knobs._rationale: PL1 averaging window. Doesn't change cap value -- only enforcement smoothing. Expected near-zero; 2 levels (half/double) to confirm.

knobs.name: CPU_POWER_TIME_WINDOW_CONTROL
knobs._rationale: RAPL PL1 averaging window. Same logic as BOARD_POWER_TIME_WINDOW.
